title: Picking a Purpose-Built Database
description:
## Massive-Scale, Low-Latency Key-Value Workload

A gaming backend needs a database for player session and profile data. The workload is **millions of simple key-value reads and writes per second**, requires **consistent single-digit-millisecond latency at any scale**, and must grow seamlessly with the player base with **minimal operational management** (no capacity planning headaches, no read-replica juggling). Queries are simple lookups by a key — there are no complex joins or ad-hoc analytical queries.

Which AWS database service BEST fits this workload?

options.text: Amazon RDS for PostgreSQL with a large instance and several read replicas
Amazon ElastiCache for Redis as the system of record

explanation: **DynamoDB** is the purpose-built choice for high-throughput key-value access: it delivers single-digit-millisecond latency at virtually any scale, is fully managed and serverless (on-demand capacity removes capacity planning), and scales horizontally without sharding or replica management. **RDS/PostgreSQL** (A) is relational and scales primarily vertically; replicas help reads but it cannot match DynamoDB's horizontal write scale or operational simplicity for millions of ops/sec. **Redshift** (C) is an OLAP data warehouse built for analytical scans over large datasets — wrong tool for high-concurrency point lookups. **ElastiCache** (D) is an in-memory cache; it is extremely fast but is not a durable system of record — using it as the primary store risks data loss on node failure/eviction.

hints.title: Match shape to engine
hints.body: Key-value lookups at massive scale = NoSQL key-value store. Joins/relations = relational. Analytics over big data = warehouse.

hints.title: Durability of a cache
hints.body: An in-memory cache is fast but volatile. Ask whether the candidate is meant to be the durable source of truth.
